articles.title: India bonds fall after US scraps Iran talks, raising escalation jitters
articles.content: Indian government bonds experienced a dip early Monday as hopes for a US-Iran diplomatic breakthrough faded. This geopolitical uncertainty, coupled with rising oil prices due to supply anxieties, is impacting import-sensitive markets like India. Investors are also closely watching global monetary policy decisions this week, while domestic swap rates remained steady.
articles.excerpt: Indian government bonds experienced a dip early Monday as hopes for a US-Iran diplomatic breakthrough faded. This geopolitical uncertainty, coupled with rising oil prices due to supply anxieties, is impacting import-sensitive markets like India. Investors are also closely watching global monetary policy decisions this week, while domestic swap rates remained steady.

articles.title: M&M Financial shares jumps 10% after Q4 profit surges 55% YoY to Rs 873 crore
articles.content: Mahindra & Mahindra Financial Services shares jumped 10% after a strong Q4 performance, with net profit rising 55% YoY to Rs 873 crore. Growth was driven by steady business momentum, margin expansion, and cost discipline. AUM grew 12%, disbursements rose 11%, and NIM expanded to 7.5%, while credit costs remained stable, underscoring operational resilience.
articles.excerpt: Mahindra & Mahindra Financial Services shares jumped 10% after a strong Q4 performance, with net profit rising 55% YoY to Rs 873 crore. Growth was driven by steady business momentum, margin expansion, and cost discipline. AUM grew 12%, disbursements rose 11%, and NIM expanded to 7.5%, while credit costs remained stable, underscoring operational resilience.

articles.title: Avoid IT, buy power and pharma: Rajesh Palviya on how to navigate volatile markets
articles.content: Indian markets face headwinds as Nifty struggles and IT stocks plummet. However, opportunities emerge in power, FMCG, and pharma sectors, according to Rajesh Palviya of Axis Securities. He advises investors to avoid IT, citing a downtrend, and instead focus on stock-specific plays like AU Small Finance Bank and SAIL for potential gains.
articles.excerpt: Indian markets face headwinds as Nifty struggles and IT stocks plummet. However, opportunities emerge in power, FMCG, and pharma sectors, according to Rajesh Palviya of Axis Securities. He advises investors to avoid IT, citing a downtrend, and instead focus on stock-specific plays like AU Small Finance Bank and SAIL for potential gains.

articles.title: Shriram Finance shares tumble 4% despite strong Q4 results: Why Nomura, other brokerages maintain 'Buy' call
articles.content: Shares of Shriram Finance fell nearly 4% on Monday despite strong Q4 FY26 results, including a 41% profit surge and healthy AUM growth. Global and domestic brokerages such as Nomura, JM Financial and Motilal Oswal Financial Services maintained ‘Buy’ calls, citing resilient fundamentals and robust medium-term growth prospects.
articles.excerpt: Shares of Shriram Finance fell nearly 4% on Monday despite strong Q4 FY26 results, including a 41% profit surge and healthy AUM growth. Global and domestic brokerages such as Nomura, JM Financial and Motilal Oswal Financial Services maintained ‘Buy’ calls, citing resilient fundamentals and robust medium-term growth prospects.

articles.title: Range-bound trend likely as investors shift focus beyond heavyweights: Narendra Solanki
articles.content: Indian markets are stabilizing as earnings season unfolds. Early IT sector results have lowered expectations. The market is now shifting focus to broader sectors and domestic growth themes. Investors are looking at mid and smallcap companies. Power, infrastructure, and autos are key areas of interest. Financials also present opportunities. The market is expected to consolidate in the near term.
articles.excerpt: Indian markets are stabilizing as earnings season unfolds. Early IT sector results have lowered expectations. The market is now shifting focus to broader sectors and domestic growth themes. Investors are looking at mid and smallcap companies. Power, infrastructure, and autos are key areas of interest. Financials also present opportunities. The market is expected to consolidate in the near term.

articles.title: Cohance Lifesciences shares skyrocket 20% on hiring former Cipla CEO Umang Vohra
articles.content: Shares of Cohance Lifesciences surged 20% on Monday after the appointment of former Cipla CEO Umang Vohra as its new Executive Chairman and Group CEO. His leadership is expected to drive transformation, scale operations, and strengthen global partnerships as the company enters its next growth phase.
articles.excerpt: Shares of Cohance Lifesciences surged 20% on Monday after the appointment of former Cipla CEO Umang Vohra as its new Executive Chairman and Group CEO. His leadership is expected to drive transformation, scale operations, and strengthen global partnerships as the company enters its next growth phase.

articles.title: Aurobindo Pharma's Rs 800 cr buyback closes this week: Should you tender shares?
articles.content: Mining giant Vedanta is set to demerge four businesses into separate listed entities, with May 1 as the record date. Shareholders will receive one share of each new company for every Vedanta share held. While listing dates are unannounced, past demergers suggest a timeline of 3 weeks to several months.
articles.excerpt: Mining giant Vedanta is set to demerge four businesses into separate listed entities, with May 1 as the record date. Shareholders will receive one share of each new company for every Vedanta share held. While listing dates are unannounced, past demergers suggest a timeline of 3 weeks to several months.

articles.title: IndusInd Bank shares jump 6%. Why Jefferies & other brokers raised their target prices
articles.content: IndusInd Bank shares surged following Q4 results that indicated a potential turnaround, with profit jumping 331% sequentially and asset quality showing signs of stabilization. Several brokerages raised their target prices, citing improved profitability and a strong recovery path, though some remain cautious about the pace of re-rating.
articles.excerpt: IndusInd Bank shares surged following Q4 results that indicated a potential turnaround, with profit jumping 331% sequentially and asset quality showing signs of stabilization. Several brokerages raised their target prices, citing improved profitability and a strong recovery path, though some remain cautious about the pace of re-rating.

articles.title: US Stock Market: DOJ ends Powell probe, prompting Tillis to greenlight Fed Chair nominee Warsh
articles.content: Senator Thom Tillis has cleared the path for Kevin Warsh's confirmation as Federal Reserve chair. This follows the Department of Justice's decision to end its probe into current Chair Jerome Powell. Warsh's nomination now moves forward, aiming for Senate approval before Powell's term ends. This development resolves months of uncertainty surrounding the crucial leadership role at the central bank.
articles.excerpt: Senator Thom Tillis has cleared the path for Kevin Warsh's confirmation as Federal Reserve chair. This follows the Department of Justice's decision to end its probe into current Chair Jerome Powell. Warsh's nomination now moves forward, aiming for Senate approval before Powell's term ends. This development resolves months of uncertainty surrounding the crucial leadership role at the central bank.

articles.title: Global Market: Japan stock rally faces test as Middle East conflict clouds earnings outlook
articles.content: Japanese companies enter earnings season on a strong footing, but Middle East tensions pose risks to growth, inflation, and energy costs. While tech optimism and reforms support equities, caution is rising. Despite resilience in earnings expectations, external uncertainties may slow momentum in what has been a strong market rally.
articles.excerpt: Japanese companies enter earnings season on a strong footing, but Middle East tensions pose risks to growth, inflation, and energy costs. While tech optimism and reforms support equities, caution is rising. Despite resilience in earnings expectations, external uncertainties may slow momentum in what has been a strong market rally.

articles.title: Global Market: Investors face triple test this week from geopolitics, rate outlook and tech earnings
articles.content: Global markets brace for a crucial week. Geopolitical tensions involving Iran and the Strait of Hormuz are a major focus. Shifting interest rate expectations from the US Federal Reserve and European central banks add to uncertainty. Big Tech earnings, especially those related to artificial intelligence, will also shape investor sentiment. This convergence of factors promises significant market movement.
articles.excerpt: Global markets brace for a crucial week. Geopolitical tensions involving Iran and the Strait of Hormuz are a major focus. Shifting interest rate expectations from the US Federal Reserve and European central banks add to uncertainty. Big Tech earnings, especially those related to artificial intelligence, will also shape investor sentiment. This convergence of factors promises significant market movement.

articles.title: Reliance Industries shares dip over 1% after Q4 results. What are Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, others saying?
articles.content: Reliance Industries shares fell over 1% after reporting a 13% YoY drop in Q4 profit despite strong revenue growth. Brokerages remain largely positive, citing recovery potential in O2C, steady retail and digital growth, and improving margins, although near-term pressures persist across segments including energy and petrochemicals.
articles.excerpt: Reliance Industries shares fell over 1% after reporting a 13% YoY drop in Q4 profit despite strong revenue growth. Brokerages remain largely positive, citing recovery potential in O2C, steady retail and digital growth, and improving margins, although near-term pressures persist across segments including energy and petrochemicals.

articles.title: Axis Bank shares tank 5% after weak Q4. What are Motilal Oswal, other top brokerages saying?
articles.content: Axis Bank shares fell after a muted Q4FY26, with net profit slipping 0.6% YoY to Rs 7,071 crore, even as both interest income and expenses rose 4.7%, weighing on investor sentiment.
articles.excerpt: Axis Bank shares fell after a muted Q4FY26, with net profit slipping 0.6% YoY to Rs 7,071 crore, even as both interest income and expenses rose 4.7%, weighing on investor sentiment.

articles.title: US Stock Market: Wall Street rally faces crucial test this week as big tech earnings and Fed decision loom
articles.content: Indian markets are watching US stocks closely. A strong rally faces key tests this week. Major companies report earnings, especially tech giants. The US Federal Reserve meets, with interest rates expected to hold steady. Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East remain a concern. Economic data will also provide insights. Investors anticipate potential market volatility.

articles.title: Bulls back on D-Street! What made Sensex jump 600 points after 3-day fall?
articles.content: Indian markets surged on Monday, ending a losing streak. Investor sentiment improved following reports of Iran sending a peace proposal to the US. Global markets also saw gains, contributing to the positive trend. Dip buying and short-covering further fueled the rebound. Despite this optimism, concerns remain regarding elevated oil prices and continued foreign investor outflows.
articles.excerpt: Indian markets surged on Monday, ending a losing streak. Investor sentiment improved following reports of Iran sending a peace proposal to the US. Global markets also saw gains, contributing to the positive trend. Dip buying and short-covering further fueled the rebound. Despite this optimism, concerns remain regarding elevated oil prices and continued foreign investor outflows.

articles.title: Silver dips Rs 1,400, gold at Rs 1.52 lakh as stalled Iran talks, rising oil prices raise rate hike fears. Key levels for today
articles.content: Gold and silver prices opened lower on MCX as rising crude oil prices and stalled U.S.-Iran talks heightened inflation and rate hike concerns. Analysts expect volatility, with key support and resistance levels identified. Despite inflation hedging traits, higher interest rates may limit gold’s upside in the near term.
articles.excerpt: Gold and silver prices opened lower on MCX as rising crude oil prices and stalled U.S.-Iran talks heightened inflation and rate hike concerns. Analysts expect volatility, with key support and resistance levels identified. Despite inflation hedging traits, higher interest rates may limit gold’s upside in the near term.

articles.title: Wipro share buyback: Here’s why retail investors should participate
articles.content: IT services major Wipro announced a Rs 15,000 crore share buyback at Rs 250 per share, a premium of over 25% to its previous closing price. Brokerages recommend participation, projecting potential net returns of 8-14% for retail investors within 2-3 months, citing high acceptance ratios based on past trends.
articles.excerpt: IT services major Wipro announced a Rs 15,000 crore share buyback at Rs 250 per share, a premium of over 25% to its previous closing price. Brokerages recommend participation, projecting potential net returns of 8-14% for retail investors within 2-3 months, citing high acceptance ratios based on past trends.

articles.title: Global Market Update: Market cap of South Korean-listed firms surpasses $4.08 trillion for 1st time
articles.content: South Korea's stock market has crossed a significant milestone, exceeding 6,000 trillion won in total capitalization. This surge is largely fueled by strong anticipation for the semiconductor sector. Major companies like Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix are performing well, contributing to the market's bullish run. Investors are focusing on upcoming corporate earnings, indicating continued market interest.

Investors are focusing on upcoming corporate earnings, indicating continued market interest.

articles.title: Bluechip safety no more? Infosys knocked out of India's top 10 valuable companies list after losing Rs 2 lakh crore
articles.content: Infosys, once a market leader, has lost significant value and exited India's top 10 companies. Investors are concerned about future growth and client spending.  Artificial intelligence presents both opportunities and challenges.  The IT sector is undergoing a transformation, impacting established players.  Infosys is adapting to these evolving market dynamics.

Artificial intelligence presents both opportunities and challenges.  The IT sector is undergoing a transformation, impacting established players.  Infosys is adapting to these evolving market dynamics.
